gdscript_logic.evidence: Built autoloads, player controller, sprite system, sim scene generator, block interaction, breaker panels, NPC system with faction AI and dialogue. Multiple state machine integrations. Complex signal wiring across systems.
failure_patterns: Autoload names not resolvable at parse time — use get_node('/root/Name') at runtime
Unused parameters trigger warnings — prefix with underscore
State machine overwrite: mid-frame state change gets clobbered — must early-return
:= fails on Variant expressions — use explicit type: 'var x: Type = expr'
add_child() during parent _ready() fails — use call_deferred()
rules_learned: Autoload load order matters — Constants before GameState before SaveManager
Type inference from Variant triggers error — use explicit types or avoid := with Variant expressions
Early-return after state transitions in _physics_process
call_deferred() for any child modification of parent during _ready()

godot_scene_structure.evidence: Built multi-system sim: 10 floors with block nodes, breaker panels, ceiling lights, stairs, NPCs as CharacterBody2D children. Collision layers: 1=player, 2=floors, 4=walls, 8=ground. Signal wiring between breaker→sim→NPC spawner→GameState.
failure_patterns: Camera2D as child of player gets flipped/rotated — keep as sibling
Nodes added to parent during its _ready() — defer the call
CharacterBody2D NPCs need their own CollisionShape2D for floor collision
Entities spawned outside floor slab bounds fall through the world
rules_learned: Separate physics body from visual node
z_index for render layering in dynamically-built scenes
Generate floor nodes in _ready() for 10+ floors
Separate collision layer (8) for ground floor — survives drop mask toggle
Zigzag stairs: two half-flights + landing per floor, one-way collision on each step

physics_feel.evidence: Sprint, charged jump/drop, somersault with charge-dependent flips all working. Jump tuned through multiple iterations: JUMP_MIN=-425 (barely clears 96px floor). Drop MIN_TIME=0.08s for single-floor tap. Sub-pixel jitter fixed with position.round().
failure_patterns: move_toward() for deceleration feels floaty — use velocity.x = 0
Jump velocity must be calculated: peak = v²/(2g), must just barely exceed FLOOR_HEIGHT
Sub-pixel positions cause pixel art shimmer — round after move_and_slide()
rules_learned: position.round() after every move_and_slide() — critical for pixel art
Camera position also needs roundf() on lerp output
Sprite queue_redraw() in _physics_process, not _process, to sync with physics
Flip thresholds: <15% = no spin, 15-70% = 1 rotation timed to airtime, 70%+ = continuous
Walk cycle: 2 distinct frames at 0.18s, no idle frames mixed in

visual_style.evidence: Player sprite with neon-yellow vest (distinct from builder NPCs). Hammer poses, lever-pull pose. Builder NPCs (orange vest, hard hat), suit NPCs (all-black), Gene (purple suit, gold tie). Speech bubbles with white PanelContainer. Industrial breaker panels with hazard stripes, ceiling lights. Font sizes tuned for readability at 2x zoom.
failure_patterns: Sin-wave limb interpolation looks uncanny — use discrete frame poses
Font size 8 is unreadable at 2x zoom — use 11+ for world text
Sub-pixel rendering causes shimmer — snap to integer positions
rules_learned: Pixel art walk cycles: 2 frames (step_a, step_b) swapped at fixed intervals
Player must be visually distinct from NPCs of same faction — neon yellow vs orange
Speech bubbles: PanelContainer + StyleBoxFlat with rounded corners + dark text on white
Industrial UI elements (breaker panels): hazard stripes, recessed panels, pivot bolts, glow effects
Ceiling lights as ColorRect rows that tween on sequentially for activation drama

narrative_design.evidence: Full dialogue pools for builders (early/mid) and suits (early/mid). User requested funnier/more compelling dialogue — rewrote with specificity and personality. Gene's lines include the faction tell slip ('the job'). Faction vocabulary rule maintained throughout.
rules_learned: Gene dialogue: if it's memorable on first read, it's too heavy. Rewrite flatter.
Suits say 'the work', builders say 'the job' — always.
Dialogue needs specificity and humor — 'I named my hammer. Her name is Brenda.' not 'The job is coming along.'
Suit tone shifts from supportive (early) to territorial (mid) — not villains, just managers

audio_design.evidence: AudioManager autoload created but no audio assets. Breaker panel and hammer mechanics designed with sound cues but no implementation yet.
audio_design.needs_from_human: Audio assets or direction on procedural audio approach

npc_system.evidence: Built full NPC system: CharacterBody2D with faction (builder/suit), idle/patrol state machine, ENTERING state for walking in from ground floor, faction-weighted floor placement, dialogue with speech bubbles, Gene as special case. Population schedule shifts suit-heavy over time.
failure_patterns: NPCs must have their own CollisionShape2D to stand on floors
NPCs spawned outside slab collision bounds fall through world
NPC sprite _draw via separate Node2D — needs _physics_process for redraw to avoid jitter
rules_learned: Spawn on floor 0 in ENTERING state, walk to stairwell, reparent to target floor
Weighted floor placement: builders cluster low, suits drift up
3 dialogue lines per NPC, pulled from phase-aware faction pool
Gene spawns as suit-looking NPC on floors 1,3,5,7 — deliberately forgettable

floor_activation.evidence: Breaker panel system: lever animation, spark particles, sequential ceiling lights, per-floor ambient particles, power cascade with block-by-block brightening. Floor 8 triggers Reckoning (stutter → blackout → enable claiming). Non-contested floors auto-claim on Reckoning start.
failure_patterns: call_deferred needed for ceiling light creation in _ready()
Cascade timers via get_tree().create_timer() — closures capture variables correctly
rules_learned: Activation is one-time, tracked in GameState.activated_floors
Lever direction: DOWN = off (red), UP = on (green) — matches real breaker panels
Sequential ceiling lights create drama without screen shake — calm activation vs violent claiming
Floor 8 Reckoning trigger: stutter cascade, flicker, blackout, then restore with claiming enabled

parallax_exterior.evidence: Multiple approaches failed: ParallaxBackground (3 iterations), manual position offsets (4 factor tuning attempts). User consistently found it 'odd'. Removed entirely. Sky/star shaders still work for altitude effects.
failure_patterns: ParallaxBackground interacts with zoom — never use it
Position-based parallax through windows looks wrong at ANY factor
Window frames create a reference grid that amplifies even tiny parallax movement
Horizontal parallax was even worse than vertical — removed immediately
rules_learned: Do NOT use position-based parallax for interior-through-windows views
Altitude-based opacity fade is the only depth effect that works here
If user says 'odd' twice about parallax, just remove it — don't keep tuning
Sky/star shaders work independently of parallax positioning
parallax_exterior.needs_from_human: Fresh approach to exterior visuals — maybe static painted backgrounds per altitude band instead of parallax

block_interaction.evidence: Full claim system with claiming_enabled gate. Blocks inert during Movement 1, activate on Reckoning. Auto-claim for non-contested floors. 8-strike hammer, border growth, sparks, completion effects.
failure_patterns: State machine overwrite bug — see f012 and rules/godot_state_machine.md
rules_learned: Block nodes replace ColorRects for interactive elements
claiming_enabled flag gates interaction — false during Movement 1, true after Reckoning
auto_claim() for bulk-claiming non-contested floors
Rapid strikes (8 x 0.075s) feel more physical than slow directional poses

tower_architecture.evidence: Tower widened from 768→2160px (BLOCK_WIDTH 64→180). Required updating constants in sim.gd, constants.gd, city_layer.gd, player spawn position, NPC bounds, breaker particle bounds, camera limits. Stairs added as zigzag stairwell.
failure_patterns: Changing BLOCK_WIDTH cascades to many hardcoded values across multiple files
FLOOR_WIDTH in city_layer.gd was a separate hardcoded constant — easy to miss
rules_learned: Derive all layout values from BLOCK_WIDTH and FLOOR_HEIGHT when possible
Player spawn, camera start, safety net teleport all use FLOOR_WIDTH/2
NPC patrol bounds scale with FLOOR_WIDTH
Stairs: zigzag with half-flights, landing at midpoint, one-way step collision

canvas2d_to_godot_porting.evidence: Established _draw() as Canvas2D replacement. Multiple systems ported successfully.
failure_patterns: Canvas save/restore → use draw_set_transform() or child nodes
Browser per-frame timing → _process(delta) and _physics_process(delta)
rules_learned: _draw() on Node2D replaces Canvas2D patterns
ColorRect for static backgrounds, _draw() for animated/conditional rendering
Collision layers replace browser's manual overlap checking
